text.tspan: Short-reach (PCB, backplane, direct attach cables, or within a rack)
Long-reach (rack-to-rack, row-to-row, or data center-to-data center)
Extremely high (up to TBps per xPU)
High, but balanced with reach (100G, 400G, 800G, moving toward 1.6T)
Ultra-low (nanoseconds) - critical for tightly coupled workloads
Low, but less stringent (microsecond scale) - acceptable for distributed workloads
Very high per node; supports massive bisection bandwidth inside a server or pod
High at cluster scale, but oversubscription common
Limited by physical node size (number of accelerators per chassis/rack)
Virtually unlimited (can connect thousands to millions of nodes)
Minimal - failure of one xPU/Switch often brings down the whole node
Mesh, ring, fully connected or custom interconnect fabrics
